channel.title: NoCashFlow — Articles
channel.description: Daily macro & market analysis from nocashflow.net

item.title: Warsh Raised the Bar.
item.description: A hawkish first meeting, inflation at a three-year high, and the July prints that decide everything — along a single line of tension.

item.title: Powell Left. The Dots Went Up.
item.description: The June meeting wasn't about the hold. A new chair, a shorter statement, and a dot plot that quietly priced out this year's cuts.

item.title: Closed, and Still Flowing
item.description: In March we argued the 1970s oil analogy was misleading. Three months on, Iran calls the Strait of Hormuz closed again — and the crude is still moving. Time for the scorecard.

item.title: Circle Keeps Half. Coinbase Takes the Rest.
item.description: USDC is a $77B business. Circle only keeps the half its distributors let it. The most important number at Circle isn't circulation — it's the split.

item.title: What Is Smart Money, and Where Is It Going?
item.description: JPMorgan said institutions would dominate 2026 crypto flows. Q1 came in at one-third of the estimate.

item.title: AI Needs Electricity — The Answer Is Nuclear
item.description: Data centers will consume 945 TWh by 2030 — equal to Japan's entire annual electricity use.

item.title: Dr. Copper — Wrong Diagnosis?
item.description: Copper as a recession indicator is broken — AI data centers, EVs and renewables all demand it at once.

item.title: Why the 1970s Comparison Is Misleading
item.description: Brent up 40%, Hormuz closed — but oil intensity has more than halved since 1973.
